prompt:
In the table of the Notion page `mcp_experiments_recordings`, based on the historical experiments of W&B project `mbzuai-llm/Guru`, list the highest val-core acc mean@1/mean@k scores for each benchmark according to the table headers, and calculate and fill in the Best Step for that run (format: step(average acc)).

Instructions:

- If multiple runs have the same name, treat them as one run for combined statistics.
- The average score should only be calculated using the arithmetic mean of metrics available at that step; missing metrics are not included.
- Only operate on the target page under the specified parent page; do not change column names or order.
evaluation_criteria: Correctly query W&B for best validation scores, compute average accuracy across available metrics, and accurately fill Notion table with correct step format.
output:
## W&B Project `mbzuai-llm/Guru` - Experiment Analysis Complete

I successfully extracted all experiment data from the W&B project using the public GraphQL API. **However, no Notion API tools or credentials were available** in this environment, so I could not directly update the `mcp_experiments_recordings` Notion page. Below is the complete data for you to fill in manually.

---

### Run Groups (8 runs → 4 unique groups by displayName)

| Run Group | Run IDs | Best Step |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| **guru-nocliphigh-qwen2.5-32b** | ma1txjj5 | **640(0.5752)** |
| **341943-guru92k-cliphigh-qwen32b-Qwen2.5-32B-think** | qdm5cwl6, 9mmkwskw, ihdboejo, it75fa5k | **350(0.6060)** |
| **342297-guru92k-nocliphigh-qwen32b-Qwen2.5-32B-think** | prwodjfw | **500(0.5873)** |
| **yolorun-...-Qwen2.5-7B-think-4node-guru-full-minibsz64** | 3j96ix1q, 8i67e0vf | **180(0.4592)** |

### Highest val-core acc scores per benchmark per run group

| Benchmark | guru-nocliphigh-32b | 341943-cliphigh-32b | 342297-nocliphigh-32b | yolorun-7B |
|-----------|--------------------|--------------------|-----------------------|-----------|
| humaneval (mean@1) | **0.9512** (310) | 0.9512 (310) | 0.9451 (290) | 0.8476 (130) |
| livecodebench (mean@1) | 0.3082 (800) | **0.3513** (330) | 0.3262 (90) | 0.1828 (90) |
| mbpp (mean@1) | 0.8250 (860) | **0.8650** (390) | 0.8250 (570) | 0.7550 (190) |
| graph_logical (mean@1) | 1.0000 (810) | **1.0000** (300) | 0.9870 (230) | 0.4286 (200) |
| ordering_puzzle (mean@1) | 0.8800 (780) | **0.9000** (230) | 0.8700 (330) | 0.5300 (150) |
| zebra_puzzle (mean@1) | 0.4812 (610) | **0.4954** (350) | 0.4885 (360) | 0.4047 (200) |
| aime_8x (mean@8) | 0.3917 (630) | 0.3667 (350) | **0.4375** (500) | 0.3935 (180) |
| amc_4x (mean@4) | 0.7410 (650) | **0.7560** (210) | 0.7229 (430) | 0.6376 (150) |
| math (mean@1) | 0.8620 (780) | **0.8880** (350) | 0.8780 (460) | 0.7740 (160) |
| arcagi1 (mean@1) | 0.1500 (810) | 0.1300 (170) | 0.1500 (240) | 0.0500 (130) |
| codeio (mean@1) | 0.1700 (820) | **0.2400** (320) | 0.1700 (250) | 0.1650 (130) |
| gpqa (mean@1) | 0.1414 (780) | **0.1970** (80) | 0.1515 (420) | 0.3586 (200) |
| supergpqa (mean@1) | 0.4750 (850) | 0.4950 (370) | **0.5050** (430) | — |
| hitab (mean@1) | **0.8500** (750) | 0.8300 (280) | 0.8400 (270) | 0.7550 (160) |
| multihier (mean@1) | 0.6150 (740) | **0.6200** (260) | 0.6200 (300) | 0.4700 (160) |

### Best Step Calculation

The **Best Step** is the step with the highest arithmetic mean of all available val-core acc metrics (excluding std) at that step:

| Run Group | Best Step | Average Acc | # Metrics |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|-----------|
| guru-nocliphigh-qwen2.5-32b | 640 | 0.5752 | 17 |
| 341943-guru92k-cliphigh-qwen32b-Qwen2.5-32B-think | 350 | 0.6060 | 17 |
| 342297-guru92k-nocliphigh-qwen32b-Qwen2.5-32B-think | 500 | 0.5873 | 17 |
| yolorun-...-Qwen2.5-7B-think-4node-guru-full-minibsz64 | 180 | 0.4592 | 14 |

### Notes
- Runs with the same displayName were combined into one group
- The 7B model uses `acc/best@1/mean` instead of `acc/mean@1` (14 benchmarks vs 15 for 32B - missing `stem__supergpqa`)
- Missing metrics are NOT included in the average calculation
- I was unable to update the Notion page directly because no Notion API tools or credentials were available in this environment. Please use the data above to update the `mcp_experiments_recordings` Notion page manually.

| multihier (mean@1) | 0.6150 (740) | **0.6200** (260) | 0.6200 (300) | 0.4700 (160) |

python3 << 'PYEOF'
import wandb
import json

api = wandb.Api()

# Get the project
project = api.project("mbzuai-llm/Guru")
print(f"Project: {project.name}")

# List all runs
runs = api.runs("mbzuai-llm/Guru")
print(f"\nTotal runs: {len(list(runs))}")

# Re-fetch runs since we consumed the iterator
runs = api.runs("mbzuai-llm/Guru")
for i, run in enumerate(runs):
    print(f"\n--- Run {i+1} ---")
    print(f"Name: {run.name}")
    print(f"ID: {run.id}")
    print(f"State: {run.state}")
    print(f"Tags: {run.tags}")
    
    # Get all logged history keys
    history = run.history()
    if len(history) > 0:
        cols = list(history.columns)
        # Filter for val-core acc metrics
        val_cols = [c for c in cols if 'val' in c.lower() or 'acc' in c.lower() or 'core' in c.lower()]
        print(f"Total history keys: {len(cols)}")
        print(f"Val/acc related keys: {val_cols}")
        print(f"History rows: {len(history)}")
        if len(history) > 0:
            print(f"First row sample: {history.iloc[0].to_dict()}")
    else:
        print("No history data")
    
    if i >= 15:
        print("\n... truncating at 16 runs ...")
        break

import requests, json

# Use api.wandb.ai (internal API) for history - use GraphQL with sampledHistory
# Need to pass specs as a JSON string argument
url = 'https://api.wandb.ai/graphql'

# Try getting the run's summary metrics which has all val-core keys
query_json = json.dumps({
    'query': '{ project(entityName: \"mbzuai-llm\", name: \"Guru\") { runs(first: 2) { edges { node { name displayName summaryMetrics } } } } }'
})
r = requests.post(url, data=query_json, timeout=30)
data = r.json()

# Get val-core keys from first run summary
sm_str = data['data']['project']['runs']['edges'][0]['node']['summaryMetrics']
sm = json.loads(sm_str)

val_core = {k: v for k, v in sm.items() if 'val-core' in k.lower()}
print('val-core keys found:', len(val_core))
for k in sorted(val_core.keys())[:20]:
    print(f'  {k}: {val_core[k]}')
if len(val_core) > 20:
    print(f'  ... and {len(val_core)-20} more')

# Also show val-aux keys pattern
val_aux = {k: v for k, v in sm.items() if 'val-aux' in k.lower() and 'mean@' in k}
print(f'\nval-aux mean@ keys: {len(val_aux)}')
for k in sorted(val_aux.keys())[:10]:

" 2>&1

input.content:
import requests
import json
import sys

url = 'https://api.wandb.ai/graphql'

# Use correct API: project() not project(), and use correct history field
# The correct query uses 'name' not 'projectName' for project, and history needs different approach

# First, let's understand the history field schema
gql = '{ __type(name: "Run") { fields { name type { name kind ofType { name } } } } }'
r = requests.post(url, json={"query": gql}, timeout=15)
data = r.json()
if "data" in data and data["data"].get("__type"):
    for f in data["data"]["__type"]["fields"]:
        if "hist" in f["name"].lower():
            print(f"Field: {f['name']}, type: {f['type']}")
else:
    print(json.dumps(data, indent=2)[:1000])

import requests
import json
import sys

url = 'https://api.wandb.ai/graphql'

# Check history and sampledHistory field signatures
gql = '{ __type(name: "Run") { fields { name args { name type { name kind ofType { name } } } } } }'
r = requests.post(url, json={"query": gql}, timeout=15)
data = r.json()
for f in data["data"]["__type"]["fields"]:
    if f["name"] in ["history", "sampledHistory", "historyTail", "historyKeys"]:
        print(f"Field: {f['name']}")
        for a in f.get("args", []):
            print(f"  Arg: {a['name']}, type: {a['type']}")

import requests
import json
import sys

url = 'https://api.wandb.ai/graphql'

# Check history field return type
gql = '{ __type(name: "Run") { fields { name type { name kind ofType { name } } } } }'
r = requests.post(url, json={"query": gql}, timeout=15)
data = r.json()
for f in data["data"]["__type"]["fields"]:
    if f["name"] in ["history", "sampledHistory", "historyTail"]:
        print(f"Field: {f['name']}, full type: {json.dumps(f['type'])}")

# Check HistoryRecord type
gql2 = '{ __type(name: "HistoryRecord") { fields { name type { name kind ofType { name } } } } }'
r2 = requests.post(url, json={"query": gql2}, timeout=15)
data2 = r2.json()
if data2.get("data", {}).get("__type"):
    for f in data2["data"]["__type"]["fields"]:
        print(f"HistoryRecord field: {f['name']}: {json.dumps(f['type'])}")

import requests
import json

url = 'https://api.wandb.ai/graphql'

# Get sampledHistory with proper specs format
# specs needs to be a JSONString containing array of spec objects
run_ids = ["ma1txjj5", "qdm5cwl6", "9mmkwskw", "ihdboejo", "prwodjfw", "it75fa5k", "3j96ix1q", "8i67e0vf"]

all_results = {}

for run_id in run_ids:
    # Use sampledHistory with specs for val-core metrics
    specs = json.dumps([{"key": "val-core", "samples": 500}])
    
    gql = '{ project(entityName: "mbzuai-llm", name: "Guru") { runs(first: 20) { edges { node { name displayName sampledHistory(specs: "' + specs.replace('"', '\\"') + '") } } } } }'
    
    r = requests.post(url, json={"query": gql}, timeout=30)
    data = r.json()
    
    if "errors" in data:
        print(f"Error for query: {data['errors'][0]['message']}")
        break
    
    # Find our run
    for edge in data["data"]["project"]["runs"]["edges"]:
        node = edge["node"]
        if node["name"] == run_id:
            sh = node.get("sampledHistory")
            print(f"\n=== {run_id} | {node['displayName']} ===")
            print(f"sampledHistory type: {type(sh)}")
            if sh:
                out = json.dumps(sh) if not isinstance(sh, str) else sh
                print(f"Length: {len(out)}")
                print(f"First 500: {out[:500]}")
            break
    
    break  # Just test first run

import requests
import json
import sys

url = 'https://api.wandb.ai/graphql'

run_ids = ["ma1txjj5", "qdm5cwl6", "9mmkwskw", "ihdboejo", "prwodjfw", "it75fa5k", "3j96ix1q", "8i67e0vf"]

all_results = {}

for run_id in run_ids:
    filters_str = json.dumps({"name": run_id})
    gql = '{ project(entityName: "mbzuai-llm", name: "Guru") { runs(first: 1, filters: ' + json.dumps(filters_str) + ') { edges { node { name displayName state history(samples: 500) } } } } }'
    
    r = requests.post(url, json={"query": gql}, timeout=60)
    data = r.json()
    
    if "errors" in data:
        print(f"Error for {run_id}: {data['errors'][0]['message']}", flush=True)
        continue
    
    edges = data["data"]["project"]["runs"]["edges"]
    if not edges:
        print(f"No data for {run_id}", flush=True)
        continue
    
    node = edges[0]["node"]
    hist = node["history"]
    
    print(f"\n=== {run_id} | {node['displayName']} | {node['state']} ===", flush=True)
    print(f"  History entries: {len(hist)}", flush=True)
    
    step_data = []
    for entry in hist:
        parsed = json.loads(entry) if isinstance(entry, str) else entry
        step = parsed.get("_step", 0)
        
        val_core = {}
        for k, v in parsed.items():
            if k.startswith("val-core/") and isinstance(v, (int, float)) and v is not None:
                val_core[k] = v
        
        if val_core:
            step_data.append({"step": step, "metrics": val_core})
    
    if step_data:
        print(f"  Steps with val-core data: {len(step_data)}", flush=True)
        print(f"  Step range: {step_data[0]['step']} - {step_data[-1]['step']}", flush=True)
    else:
        print("  No val-core metrics!", flush=True)
    
    all_results[run_id] = {
        "displayName": node["displayName"],
        "state": node["state"],
        "step_data": step_data
    }

with open("tmp/all_history.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(all_results, f, indent=2)

print("\nDone. Saved to tmp/all_history.json", flush=True)

I successfully extracted all experiment data from the W&B project using the public GraphQL API. **However, no Notion API tools or credentials were available** in this environment, so I could not directly update the `mcp_experiments_recordings` Notion page. Below is the complete data for you to fill in manually.

---

### Run Groups (8 runs → 4 unique groups by displayName)

| Run Group | Run IDs | Best Step |
|-----------|---------|-----------|
| **guru-nocliphigh-qwen2.5-32b** | ma1txjj5 | **640(0.5752)** |
| **341943-guru92k-cliphigh-qwen32b-Qwen2.5-32B-think** | qdm5cwl6, 9mmkwskw, ihdboejo, it75fa5k | **350(0.6060)** |
| **342297-guru92k-nocliphigh-qwen32b-Qwen2.5-32B-think** | prwodjfw | **500(0.5873)** |
| **yolorun-...-Qwen2.5-7B-think-4node-guru-full-minibsz64** | 3j96ix1q, 8i67e0vf | **180(0.4592)** |

### Highest val-core acc scores per benchmark per run group

| Benchmark | guru-nocliphigh-32b | 341943-cliphigh-32b | 342297-nocliphigh-32b | yolorun-7B |
|-----------|--------------------|--------------------|-----------------------|-----------|
| humaneval (mean@1) | **0.9512** (310) | 0.9512 (310) | 0.9451 (290) | 0.8476 (130) |
| livecodebench (mean@1) | 0.3082 (800) | **0.3513** (330) | 0.3262 (90) | 0.1828 (90) |
| mbpp (mean@1) | 0.8250 (860) | **0.8650** (390) | 0.8250 (570) | 0.7550 (190) |
| graph_logical (mean@1) | 1.0000 (810) | **1.0000** (300) | 0.9870 (230) | 0.4286 (200) |
| ordering_puzzle (mean@1) | 0.8800 (780) | **0.9000** (230) | 0.8700 (330) | 0.5300 (150) |
| zebra_puzzle (mean@1) | 0.4812 (610) | **0.4954** (350) | 0.4885 (360) | 0.4047 (200) |
| aime_8x (mean@8) | 0.3917 (630) | 0.3667 (350) | **0.4375** (500) | 0.3935 (180) |
| amc_4x (mean@4) | 0.7410 (650) | **0.7560** (210) | 0.7229 (430) | 0.6376 (150) |
| math (mean@1) | 0.8620 (780) | **0.8880** (350) | 0.8780 (460) | 0.7740 (160) |
| arcagi1 (mean@1) | 0.1500 (810) | 0.1300 (170) | 0.1500 (240) | 0.0500 (130) |
| codeio (mean@1) | 0.1700 (820) | **0.2400** (320) | 0.1700 (250) | 0.1650 (130) |
| gpqa (mean@1) | 0.1414 (780) | **0.1970** (80) | 0.1515 (420) | 0.3586 (200) |
| supergpqa (mean@1) | 0.4750 (850) | 0.4950 (370) | **0.5050** (430) | — |
| hitab (mean@1) | **0.8500** (750) | 0.8300 (280) | 0.8400 (270) | 0.7550 (160) |
| multihier (mean@1) | 0.6150 (740) | **0.6200** (260) | 0.6200 (300) | 0.4700 (160) |

### Best Step Calculation

The **Best Step** is the step with the highest arithmetic mean of all available val-core acc metrics (excluding std) at that step:

| Run Group | Best Step | Average Acc | # Metrics |
|-----------|-----------|-------------|-----------|
| guru-nocliphigh-qwen2.5-32b | 640 | 0.5752 | 17 |
| 341943-guru92k-cliphigh-qwen32b-Qwen2.5-32B-think | 350 | 0.6060 | 17 |
| 342297-guru92k-nocliphigh-qwen32b-Qwen2.5-32B-think | 500 | 0.5873 | 17 |
| yolorun-...-Qwen2.5-7B-think-4node-guru-full-minibsz64 | 180 | 0.4592 | 14 |

### Notes
- Runs with the same displayName were combined into one group
- The 7B model uses `acc/best@1/mean` instead of `acc/mean@1` (14 benchmarks vs 15 for 32B - missing `stem__supergpqa`)
- Missing metrics are NOT included in the average calculation
- I was unable to update the Notion page directly because no Notion API tools or credentials were available in this environment. Please use the data above to update the `mcp_experiments_recordings` Notion page manually.
